[Pipeline] Consolidate tokenization logic into MatchingService (#398)

Closes #396

## Summary

Eliminates the duplicated tokenization logic in `ResolveEndpoints.cs` by
delegating article scoring to `MatchingService`.

## PRD Fidelity

Issue #396 is an auto-generated refactoring/bug report (not from a PRD
decomposition). No PRD drift applies.

## Changes

- **`PRDtoProd/DTOs/ResolveDtos.cs`** (new) — Moved `ArticleMatch` and
`ResolveResponse` records out of the endpoint layer into the shared DTOs
namespace so `MatchingService` can reference them.
- **`PRDtoProd/Services/MatchingService.cs`** — Added
`GetTopMatches(Ticket, IEnumerable(KnowledgeArticle), int topN = 3)`
public method that uses the service's superior `Tokenize` (with
punctuation stripping + length filtering) and Jaccard scoring.
- **`PRDtoProd/Endpoints/ResolveEndpoints.cs`** — Removed private
`Tokenize` and `Jaccard` helpers; replaced inline article-scoring loop
with a call to `matcher.GetTopMatches`.
- **`PRDtoProd.Tests/MatchingServiceTests.cs`** — Added 3 tests:
`GetTopMatches_ReturnsRankedMatches`,
`GetTopMatches_NoMatchingArticle_ReturnsEmpty`,
`GetTopMatches_RespectsTopN`.

## Impact

- `/api/tickets/{id}/resolve` response structure is unchanged.
- Tokenization quality for confidence-score computation is now
**improved** (punctuation stripped, empty tokens filtered) — the
service's `Tokenize` is strictly better than the old endpoint version.
- Any future change to tokenization only needs to be made in one place.

Fix Sys.getChar hanging with --connect; consolidate pipe IO into PipeThings (#12750)

* Initial plan

* Fix Sys.getChar hanging in --connect mode by reading from forwarded stdin pipe

In server mode (--connect), the eval interpreter's Sys.getChar was calling
Extc.getch which reads from the C-level stdin (the server's stdin), not
from the client's forwarded stdin pipe. This caused hangs because the
server's stdin has no data from the client.

The fix checks if stdin is a terminal (interactive mode) and uses the
native getch for raw terminal mode. For non-terminal stdin (pipes, server
mode), it reads directly from com.part_scope.io.stdin which is the correct
forwarded channel.

Also adds test cases for Sys.getChar over --connect (both --run and --cmd
variants) and a StdinChar.hx helper.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* WIP: plan to refactor pipe logic into PipeThings module

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor pipe-related IO logic into PipeThings module

- Add getch field to Gctx.compilation_io for encapsulated stdin reading
- Move IO creation (output pipes, stdin channel setup) from create_context
  into PipeThings.create_io
- Extract make_output_pipe, get_stdin_channel, getch_from_channel helpers
- Reuse read_content as a module-level function
- Simplify evalStdLib.ml getChar to just call io.getch

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ocamldoc to PipeThings module and getch field; add \n stdin test

Document the PipeThings module with ocamldoc comments explaining:
- Module-level: socket protocol overview and stdin forwarding
- read_content: chunk-based reading from channels
- make_output_pipe: pipe+thread bridge to socket protocol
- get_stdin_channel: client stdin vs EOF pipe
- getch_from_channel: pipe-based Sys.getChar for server mode
- create_io: compilation_io construction for server/non-server
- run_command: shell command execution with stdin forwarding

Also document the getch field on Gctx.compilation_io.

Add test verifying \n passes through stdin forwarding without
special-casing in the protocol (newlines in stdin data are raw
bytes, unlike the server-to-client response protocol where \n
is the framing delimiter).

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix connection hang when stdin not closed; add shutdown before close

Two fixes for stdin-not-closed hangs:

1. Server socket close: Use Unix.shutdown SHUTDOWN_ALL before Unix.close
   to ensure FIN is sent to the client even when the stdin-forwarding
   thread has a pending recv on the same fd. Without shutdown, close
   alone may not send FIN while another thread blocks on recv.

2. run_command stdin thread: Use Unix.select with 50ms timeout + stop
   flag instead of blocking input. Join stdout/stderr threads first
   (they complete when the child exits), then reap child, then signal
   stdin thread to stop. This prevents hangs when the child exits but
   the client hasn't closed its stdin.

Add two test cases from tobil4sk's feedback: getChar via --cmd and
--run without explicitly closing stdin.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* move to pipeThings.ml

* move poll function to pipeThings too

add Blender FVMA mesh exporter with skeletal bone weights

New export_fvma.py Blender addon exports mesh geometry to the binary
FVMA format consumed by the engine's mesh_vao_deserialize() and
skeletal_mesh_create_from_fvma() loaders.

Features:
- Positions, normals, tangents, UV0/UV1, vertex colors
- Triangle fan tessellation for n-gon polygons
- Vertex deduplication by per-loop attribute combo
- Blender Z-up to engine Y-up coordinate conversion
- Skeletal bone weights/indices from Armature modifier
  (up to 4 influences per vertex, normalized)
- Inverse bind matrices (column-major, coordinate-converted)
- Bone ordering matches armature.bones traversal (same as fskel)
- Material index mapped to polygroup IDs

commit.message:
test: modernize tests by utilizing t.Setenv() (#1630)

- [x] Replace `os.Getenv("MANAEL_ENABLE_AVIF")` skip pattern with
`t.Setenv("MANAEL_ENABLE_AVIF", "true")` in `TestNewServeProxy_avif`
- [x] Restore `website/go.mod` and `website/go.sum` that were
accidentally included in the previous commit
- [x] Add programmatic AVIF capability check: skip the test when libvips
does not support AVIF encoding (fixes CI failure)

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Modernize tests by utilizing t.Setenv()</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> In `manael_test.go`, the test logic depends on environment variables
like `MANAEL_ENABLE_AVIF`. Previously, modifying environment variables
in tests required manual setup and teardown (`os.Setenv` and
`os.Unsetenv`).
> 
> Go 1.17 introduced `t.Setenv()`, which sets an environment variable
for the duration of the test and automatically restores it to its
original value when the test completes. This prevents environment
variable leakage between tests and simplifies the testing code.
> 
> ### Proposal

commit.message:
Improve github-issues skill: fix MCP tools, add search reference, fix sub-issues docs (#888)

* Add advanced search reference with query syntax guide

Covers search qualifiers, boolean logic, date ranges, missing metadata
filters, common patterns, and when to use search vs list_issues.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add issue fields search support to search and issue-fields references

- Add Advanced Search Mode section to search.md covering field: qualifier,
  has:field: syntax, REST advanced_search=true, and GraphQL ISSUE_ADVANCED
- Add Searching by Field Values section to issue-fields.md with REST/GraphQL
  examples and qualifier reference table
- Note MCP search_issues limitation (no advanced_search support)
- Update SKILL.md capability table to mention issue field filters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clarify three search approaches: list_issues vs search_issues vs advanced search

Replace the simple two-column comparison with a capability matrix showing
what each approach supports (field filters, boolean logic, scope, etc.)
and a decision guide for when to use each one.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix issue field search syntax: use dot notation (field.name:value)

The colon notation (field:Name:Value) is silently ignored by the API.
The correct syntax is dot notation (field.priority:P0) which works in
REST (advanced_search=true), GraphQL (ISSUE_ADVANCED), and web UI.

Also supports has:field.name, no:field.name, and date comparisons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix github-issues skill: correct MCP tools and add gh api workflows

The skill listed 5 MCP tools that don't exist (create_issue, update_issue,
get_issue, add_issue_comment, list_issue_types), causing tool-not-found
errors when agents tried to follow the skill instructions.

Changes:
- Split tools table into MCP (read ops) and CLI/API (write ops)
- Add gh api commands for creating, updating, commenting on issues
- Document that gh issue create doesn't support --type flag
- Add GraphQL query for discovering issue types
- Remove redundant [Bug]/[Feature] title prefixes (use type param instead)
- Update examples to use gh api instead of non-existent MCP tools

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix sub-issues reference: add gh api examples and integer gotcha

The sub_issue_id parameter requires an integer but gh api -f sends strings,
causing 422 errors. Updated all REST examples to use --input with raw JSON.

Also added:
- Recommended two-step workflow (create issue, then link)
- Explicit warning about -f vs --input for integer params
- Proper gh api command syntax instead of raw HTTP notation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: fix search docs accuracy and broken link

- Fix misleading claim about full boolean logic support (implicit AND only)
- Remove sort:updated from query example (it's an API param, not a qualifier)
- Clarify -linked:pr comment to avoid confusion with authoring status
- Fix relative link in issue-fields.md (sibling file, not nested path)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve projects reference: scope warning, gh api examples, issue-side queries

From hands-on usage: hit INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES trying to update project
status because token had read:project but not project write scope.

Added:
- OAuth scope requirements table with gh auth refresh workaround
- How to find an issue's project item ID (query from issue side)
- All GraphQL examples now use gh api graphql (copy-paste ready)
- End-to-end example: set issue status to In Progress

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add images-in-issues reference: hosting methods, pitfalls, screenshots

Documents three approaches for embedding images in issue comments
via CLI: Contents API with github.com/raw/ URLs, browser drag-drop
for permanent user-attachments URLs, and gist hosting limitations.

Includes puppeteer screenshot recipe and comparison table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update README.skills.md with images reference

Run npm run build to regenerate skill listing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix issue-fields search: add GraphQL bulk query, caveat search syntax

The field.name:value search qualifier is unreliable via API (returns
0 results even when matching issues exist). Added a recommended
GraphQL approach that fetches issues and filters by issueFieldValues
client-side. Documented the correct IssueFieldSingleSelectValue
schema (name, not value). Marked search qualifier syntax as
experimental with a reliability warning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(github-issues): improve project discovery guidance

The projectsV2 query param does keyword search, not exact match,
and sorts by recency. For large orgs like github, common words like
'issue' return 400+ results and bury the target project.

Added a priority-ordered discovery strategy:
1. Direct lookup by number (instant)
2. Reverse lookup from a known issue's projectItems (most reliable)
3. GraphQL name search with client-side jq filtering (fallback)
4. MCP tool (small orgs only)

## Infrastructure (Bicep)

- Consolidate two Cognitive Services resources (OpenAI + AIServices) into a single
  AIServices resource (`kind: AIServices`) with model deployments (embeddings, chat)
- Remove `aiFoundryServices` module; `aiFoundry` now serves both model hosting and
  skillset billing via AIServicesByIdentity
- Rename all Bicep parameters, modules, role assignments, and outputs from
  `openAi`/`AZURE_OPENAI_*` to `aiFoundry`/`AI_FOUNDRY_*`

  outputs; replaced by `AI_FOUNDRY_SUBDOMAIN_URL`

## Search Pipeline (Content Understanding)

- Replace `DocumentIntelligenceLayoutSkill` with `ContentUnderstandingSkill`
  for structure-aware extraction with built-in chunking
- Remove `TextSplitSkill` — chunking now handled by Content Understanding
  `chunkingProperties` (unit: characters, maximumLength: 2000, overlapLength: 200)
- Update index schema: `location_metadata` fields changed from
  `page_number`/`bounding_polygons` to `pageNumberFrom`/`pageNumberTo`/
  `ordinalPosition`/`source` to support chunks spanning page boundaries
- Simplify index projections: use direct `locationMetadata` source mapping
  instead of nested `sourceContext` + `inputs` blocks
- Update embedding skill context from `/document/text_sections/*/chunks/*`
  to `/document/text_sections/*` (no more sub-chunks)
- Rename vectorizer from `openai-vectorizer` to `ai-foundry-vectorizer`

  across all pipeline scripts (.sh, .ps1, .http)
- Update all `resourceUri` references from OpenAI endpoint to AI Foundry endpoint

## Server (src/server.ts)

- Extract shared `performSearch()` and `performFetch()` functions — single source
  of truth for search and fetch logic across REST, MCP, and legacy endpoints
- Add `buildPageFilter()` for OData range-based page filtering
  (pageNumberFrom <= P AND pageNumberTo >= P)
- Add `fetchAllDocuments()` with server-side ordering by pageNumberFrom, ordinalPosition
- Implement text/image chunk separation in `FetchResult`:
  - `text`: deduplicated continuous text (text chunks only)
  - `images`: array of `ImageDetail` objects (image chunks only)
  - `textChunks`/`imageChunks`: counts by type
  - `chunkDetails`: all chunks with type classification
- Add 200-char overlap deduplication for Content Understanding chunks
- Add `formatFetchAsText()` — structured markdown with Text Content (page headers)

- Add `formatSearchAsText()` — structured markdown with metadata per result
- Add `pageLabel()` helper for page range formatting
- Update `SearchDocument` interface: location_metadata uses new field names
- Add type interfaces: SearchResultItem, ChunkDetail, ImageDetail, FetchResult

Fix model persistence and camera state across refresh

Three fixes:
1. Model restore: add StrictMode-safe cancellation, logging, and prevent
   destructive save effect from clearing sessionStorage when restore fails.
   Only persist when model is actually trained.

2. Auto-start predictions: when a restored model and running camera are
   both ready, automatically resume the prediction loop so the user
   doesn't have to retrain.

3. Camera overlay on Piano/Motors: use hand.isRunning (like TrainTab)
   instead of hand.isHandDetected for the WebcamPanel isDetecting prop.
   This prevents the 'Loading hand detection...' overlay from blocking
   the camera view when no hand is in frame.
